content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 20, 1990

George Johnson
Lompoc Realty
410 E. Ocean Avenue
Lompoc, CA 93456

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
     Our File No. I-90-321

Dear Mr. Johnson:

     This is in response to your letter of May 1, 1990 requesting
advice from this agency as a potential candidate for elective of-
fice in the City of Lompoc. The Fair Political Practices Commis-
sion provides advice with respect to the provisions of the Politi-
cal Reform Act (the "Act").¹ This letter therefore addresses your
inquiry only with respect to the act; you are advised to contact
your city attorney's office for further information concerning
other provisions.

QUESTION

     You have inquired whether you may run for more than one city
elective office in the November 1990 election.

CONCLUSION

     The Act does not prohibit you from seeking election or being
elected to multiple offices. However, because other legal provi-
sions could impact on your situation, you should contact your city
attorney as to those matters, as discussed in this letter.

FACTS

     In the November 1990 election the City of Lompoc will be con-
ducting an election for the office of mayor and for three city
council offices. There will actually be three separate elections:
(1) the mayoral election, for a two year term; (2) two council
offices, each for four year terms; and (3) a special election to
fill the remaining two years of a council office which has become

________________
     ¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Di-
vision 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Our File No. I-90-321
Page 2

vacant. You are interested in running in all three separate
elections, but have been informed by the city clerk that such was
not possible.

ANALYSIS

There are no provisions of the Act which either preclude an
individual from seeking more than one elective office in a single
election, or from serving in more than one elective office at any
one time. The Act's campaign, disclosure, and conflict of inter-
est provisions would apply to each candidacy/office sought and
held.2

Moreover, since salary from a local government agency is not
"income" under the Act (Section 82030(b)(2)), running for or hold-
ing multiple local offices would not alone give rise to a conflict
of interest under the Act. (Sections 87100, 87103(c).)

The advice provided by the Fair Political Practices Commis-
sion is limited to the provisions of the Act. Other legal provi-
sions, such as Government Code Section 1090, the general doctrine
of incompatible offices, various Election Code sections, or any
local ordinances, all may have some bearing on your situation but
are beyond the scope of this letter. You should contact your city
attorney for advice as to these matters.

I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this mat-
ter, please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Jonathan S. Rothman
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JSR:plh

_______________________
2 Because the Act's contribution limitations (Sections 85300-
85307) restrict contributions from single sources per fiscal year,
the same single limit would apply regardless of how many offices
were sought during a particular election.
